meta.tagline: AI-ready platform for multi-party discussion, structured analysis, reusable knowledge, and graph-ready reasoning
meta.description: Dialectica is a model-agnostic platform for multi-party debate, discussion, meeting capture, argument structuring, controversy tracking, AI-assisted summarization and evaluation, knowledge extraction, and knowledge graph prototyping.

nav.notificationDoNotDisturbHint: New local notifications are suppressed while this is on.

common.aiDisclaimer: AI can make mistakes. Please verify important information.

roomManage.title: Room Management
roomManage.joinModeApprovalHint: New members must be approved by the owner or admin.
roomManage.kickConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this participant?
roomManage.transferConfirm: Are you sure you want to transfer room ownership? You will become an admin.
roomManage.destroyConfirm: Are you sure you want to archive this room? Members will keep chat history but can no longer post.
roomManage.roomArchived: This room has been archived. Messages are read-only.
roomManage.nicknamePlaceholder: Enter a nickname (only visible to you)
roomManage.ownerOnly: Only the room owner can perform this action.
roomManage.adminOnly: Only admins or the room owner can perform this action.

roomAi.automationBody: Manage manual and automatic project summaries without blocking on-demand AI help.
roomAi.modeAutoHint: Auto mode runs summaries, evaluations, and follow-up prompts after the room reaches the thresholds you set.
roomAi.modeManualHint: Manual mode keeps automation off, but you can still ask the room AI to jump in on demand.
roomAi.modeBasicHint: Automatically saves a summary after a fixed number of new messages. Manual summary requests always remain available.
roomAi.modeAssistiveHint: For multi-user rooms, AI reviews each threshold batch and saves a summary only when the batch has enough signal; low-signal batches are marked processed and only adjust the next threshold.
roomAi.modeOffHint: Automatic summaries are off. You can still request a summary manually at any time.
roomAi.responseStyleHint: This controls how the room AI speaks when you ask for a manual summary or let it join the discussion automatically.
roomAi.summaryThresholdHint: In automatic mode, this fixed count triggers and saves the next summary.
roomAi.assistiveThresholdHint: In assistive mode, this count triggers an AI review. The summary is saved only when the batch has enough signal.
roomAi.assistiveThresholdCurrentHint: This is the active review threshold AI is currently using for the next multi-user batch.
roomAi.noApiKey: No AI API key configured for this room
roomAi.facilitatorTriggerLabel: Admins can trigger AI actions manually
roomAi.permissionsOwnerOnly: Only the host can decide whether admins get these automation permissions.
roomAi.manageAutomationGranted: Your current role can manage automation in this room.
roomAi.manageAutomationLocked: Your current role can review automation rules, but cannot change them.
roomAi.summaryAutomationBody: Summary history stays linear. Automatic summaries never block manual AI requests.
roomAi.summaryAutomationHint: When the threshold is reached, the system will automatically create a fresh discussion summary.
roomAi.evaluationAutomationHint: When the threshold is reached, the system will automatically run a quality evaluation of the discussion.
roomAi.followupAutomationHint: When the threshold is reached, the system will automatically suggest next-step follow-up questions.

dashboard.heroBadge: One live context for people, structured reasoning, and AI analysis
dashboard.heroTitle: Turn every live discussion into a reasoning map your team and AI can keep building on.
dashboard.heroBody: Dialectica keeps room messages, timeline entries, argument structure, attachments, and AI analysis on the same evolving workspace so discussion does not stop at the meeting itself.
dashboard.heroHint: Move directly from live discussion into reusable knowledge, graph relations, and follow-on AI collaboration.
dashboard.batchDeleteConfirm: Delete {count} project(s)? This cannot be undone.
dashboard.recentProjectsHint: Resume an active room, review a bundled example, or open a new AI-ready discussion workspace.
dashboard.emptyBody: Create a workspace, import an existing transcript, or launch one of the bundled examples to explore live capture, argument mapping, provider settings, and reporting.
dashboard.workflowOneBody: Record speaker turns, timestamps, tags, ownership, and room presence as the conversation unfolds.
dashboard.workflowTwoBody: Link claims, evidence, rebuttals, questions, conclusions, and action items into a navigable reasoning map.
dashboard.workflowThreeBody: Pass structured room state, participants, goals, and transcript context into provider adapters for summarization and evaluation.
dashboard.highlightOne: AI workspace keeps private chats, attachments, and model replies.
dashboard.highlightTwo: Project workspaces organize goals, entries, structure, and reports.
dashboard.highlightThree: Collaboration rooms track roles, invites, permissions, and live context.
dashboard.highlightFour: Automatic and assistive summaries filter key points before saving.
dashboard.highlightFive: Knowledge Hub gathers nodes, topic clusters, and source links.
dashboard.highlightSix: 2D / 3D graphs show claims, evidence, risks, and conclusions.
dashboard.actionNewBody: Start a new debate, discussion, meeting, or negotiation room with structured metadata.
dashboard.actionImportBody: Import an exported project backup, or turn Markdown / TXT notes and source material into a structured workspace.
dashboard.actionSettingsBody: Apply language, appearance, provider, privacy, and collaboration preferences from a single Settings control surface.
dashboard.actionSampleBody: Browse richer bundled rooms covering debate, meeting, and negotiation patterns.
dashboard.aiReadyTitle: Provider surface prepared for mainstream AI platforms
dashboard.aiReadyBody: Dialectica ships with a local rule engine by default, plus server-side HTTP adapter routes that can call mainstream provider APIs when valid credentials and endpoints are configured. Discussion outputs can then flow into the knowledge hub and graph prototype.
dashboard.actionKnowledgeBody: Review extracted knowledge nodes, categories, topics, and provenance across every discussion.
dashboard.actionGraphBody: Trace how topics, viewpoints, evidence, conflicts, and conclusions connect across projects.
dashboard.workflowFourBody: Generate reusable knowledge nodes, preserve provenance, and map relationships into an evolving graph view.
dashboard.visibilitySummaryBody: Public rooms appear in the dashboard preview, invite-only rooms are joined through links, and private rooms stay scoped to the current workspace members.
dashboard.publicRoomsEmpty: There are no public rooms yet. Once a workspace is marked public, it will appear here as a shared discussion space.
dashboard.actionSoloBody: Start a private AI conversation thread like ChatGPT first, then open it as a shared room when you actually need collaboration.

projectList.title: Discussion workspaces
projectList.subtitle: Manage debate, discussion, meeting, and negotiation rooms from one dashboard.

project.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the decision context, topic scope, and what the AI analysis layer should help clarify.
project.goalPlaceholder: State the target outcome, decision boundary, or question this room should resolve.

overviewCard.title: Workspace briefing
overviewCard.subtitle: Track scenario, objective, provider state, room status, and structural progress at a glance.

participantsCard.title: Participant roster
participantsCard.subtitle: Manage debate roles, collaboration roles, positions, seat labels, colors, and room presence.
participantsCard.addSeatHint: Add a seat for a debate role or discussion position. For inviting real users, use the invite system in the collaboration tab.
participantsCard.inviteRealUsers: To invite real users, go to the Collaboration tab and create an invite link.
participantsCard.name: Name
participantsCard.empty: No participants yet. Add the people in the room before recording the conversation.
participantsCard.keepOne: Keep at least one participant in the workspace.
participantsCard.readOnly: Participant profiles in bundled samples are read-only so the room structure remains intact.
participantsCard.hostNote: The host profile is tied to local identity, room permissions, and invite behavior. Save after editing so the workspace stays in sync.
participantsCard.saveHint: Save participant changes to update room presence, message ownership, and timeline metadata.
participantsCard.profileManagedLocal: This participant is bound to the local profile on this device. Update display name or avatar from Settings.
participantsCard.profileManagedRemote: This participant comes from an invited member profile. Room roles and presence can change here, but their own name and avatar stay owned by that remote profile.
participantsCard.removeHostLocked: The current local host profile cannot be removed directly. Assign another host first or change the local profile binding.
participantsCard.presenceLocked: You can only change presence for identities you manage, unless the current room host updates it for the room.
participantsCard.selfRoomManaged: This seat is bound to your current local profile. Display name and avatar come from Settings, while room notes and presence can still be adjusted here.
participantsCard.remoteParticipantLocked: This participant is managed by another member profile. The current identity can inspect it, but cannot overwrite their profile details.
participantsCard.removeLocked: Your current identity cannot remove this participant. Use a host or facilitator profile, or transfer host ownership first.
participantsCard.timelineLocked: This participant already owns timeline messages and cannot be removed directly, so the history remains trustworthy.

captureCard.title: Live capture
captureCard.subtitle: Capture manual turns, split pasted paragraphs, preserve ownership, and track sync state while the room is active.
captureCard.speaker: Speaker
captureCard.contentPlaceholder: Capture a statement, question, rebuttal, clarification, or stage summary.
captureCard.empty: No transcript entries yet. The live timeline appears here once the discussion starts.
captureCard.bulkHint: Paste long notes and let Dialectica split them into sequential entries for faster live capture.
captureCard.liveRoomBody: Shared room messages update the room feed first. They are then written back into the project timeline so history, AI analysis, and knowledge extraction stay in sync.
captureCard.syncHintTitle: Realtime capture syncs into the room and the timeline
captureCard.syncHintBody: Entries created here are written into the project timeline, the shared room history, and the context chain used by later AI analysis.
captureCard.recorded: The message was written to the shared room and timeline.
captureCard.recentMomentsBody: The latest turns written into the shared room appear here so you can confirm the room feed, timeline, and AI context stay aligned.
captureCard.joinBeforeCapture: Join this public room before writing live turns into the timeline.
captureCard.captureLocked: Your current identity does not have a room seat that can write into the timeline.

timelineCard.title: Timeline
timelineCard.subtitle: Search, filter, highlight keywords, review ownership, and jump from raw turns into structured nodes.
timelineCard.keywordHint: Keyword highlighting follows the global preference in Settings.
timelineCard.empty: No timeline items match the current filters.

structureCard.title: Argument structure
structureCard.subtitle: Organize claims, evidence, rebuttals, questions, clarifications, assumptions, conclusions, and action items into an explicit reasoning graph.
structureCard.emptyNodes: No argument nodes yet. Create nodes from transcript entries or add them manually.
structureCard.emptyRelations: No node relations yet. Connect supporting and rebutting logic to reveal the reasoning chain.

insightsCard.title: Controversies, gaps, and consensus
insightsCard.subtitle: Track unresolved questions, evidence gaps, repeated points, provisional agreements, and pending issues the AI layer should inspect.
insightsCard.empty: No insight items yet. Save the workspace or switch providers to refresh structured analysis.

summaryCard.title: Summary and evaluation
summaryCard.subtitle: Review AI-ready summaries, disputes, unresolved questions, draft evaluations, and next-step guidance in one panel.

summaryHistory.title: Summary history
summaryHistory.hint: Keep each manual or automatic summary as a linear history so the workspace can be reviewed in stages.

project.attachmentsViewInCollab: View and manage attachments in the collaboration tab, or use the API to browse all attachments.
project.attachmentsLoadFailed: Unable to load attachments right now. Please retry.
project.deleteAttachmentConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this attachment?
project.auditLoadFailed: Unable to load the activity log right now. Please retry.
project.noProjectsToLink: No other projects available to link.
project.linkedProjectsHint: Link related projects to view them together in the knowledge graph.
project.linkedProjectIdPlaceholder: Enter a project ID and press Enter

roomCard.title: Room and collaboration state
roomCard.subtitle: Keep the collaboration model explicit even before full real-time networking is connected.
roomCard.empty: No presence records yet. Room/session scaffolding is ready for future live sync adapters.
roomCard.presenceHidden: Presence indicators are hidden for this workspace because they are disabled in Settings.
roomCard.autoAnalysisGuide: Auto summary and auto evaluation only auto-run in the local rule engine / local-only analysis mode. Remote providers stay manual to avoid silent usage and spend.

workspaceSettings.title: Workspace controls
workspaceSettings.subtitle: Save the room, export artifacts, trigger provider tasks, and review the latest orchestration snapshot.
workspaceSettings.templateSaveHint: Saves this project's structure, goals, and safe settings, without history, attachments, graphs, or keys.
workspaceSettings.runSummaryHint: AI summarizes the core content, conclusions, and next steps of the current discussion.
workspaceSettings.runEvaluationHint: AI evaluates the strengths, gaps, and logical quality of each participant's arguments.
workspaceSettings.runFollowupHint: AI suggests follow-up questions, unresolved issues, and concrete action items.
workspaceSettings.providerSubtitle: This panel shows which provider, model, and task last touched the summary surface.
workspaceSettings.roomAiSubtitle: A shared room always uses one active provider / model / key set at a time, controlled by the host or facilitator.
workspaceSettings.roomAiUsingLocalProfile: This room is currently using the AI configuration saved in your profile. When other members trigger AI tasks here, Dialectica still uses your room-level configuration.
workspaceSettings.roomAiUsingHostProfile: This room is currently bound to the AI configuration saved by {name}. Other members can trigger AI tasks, but their own keys are not mixed in automatically.
workspaceSettings.useCurrentProviderForRoom: Use my current settings for this room
workspaceSettings.roomAiControllerLocked: Only the room host or facilitator can change the active AI configuration for the room.
workspaceSettings.taskCompleted: AI output has been updated in this workspace's insights and summary panels.
workspaceSettings.goalGuide: When a discussion goal is set, AI keeps the conversation, summaries, and follow-ups aligned to that goal. If left empty, AI follows the conversation naturally.
workspaceSettings.languageGuide: Workspace language drives the default language for AI replies, summaries, evaluations, follow-ups, and extracted knowledge.

collaborationPanel.title: Shared collaboration room
collaborationPanel.subtitle: Keep the live room, presence, attachments, and AI interventions aligned in one shared discussion surface.
collaborationPanel.invitesDisabled: Invite creation is disabled in Settings for this workspace.
collaborationPanel.noInvites: No invite links have been created yet.
collaborationPanel.messagePlaceholder: Write the next discussion message, facilitator note, or coordination handoff to the shared room.
collaborationPanel.noAttachments: No attachments yet. Documents, images, videos, and file references appear here.
collaborationPanel.presenceSyncFailed: Presence sync failed. The room stays usable; we'll retry on the next status update.
collaborationPanel.sharedRoomBody: Everyone enters the same group discussion room with message history, system events, attachments, and AI analysis preserved as shared context.
collaborationPanel.historyVisible: New members can review the shared timeline, system events, and AI interventions from earlier in the room.
collaborationPanel.acceptInviteBody: Paste an invite token to join this shared room and inherit the current discussion history.
collaborationPanel.inviteAccepted: Invite accepted. The participant joined the shared room and can review the full history.
collaborationPanel.roomPulseBody: This room keeps one shared timeline for people, AI outputs, attachments, and system events so nobody loses context after joining.
collaborationPanel.feedBody: The full shared history lives here, including participant messages, AI interventions, joins, invites, and attachment references.
collaborationPanel.latestAiEmpty: No AI summary or recommendation has been posted to the room yet.
collaborationPanel.composeBody: Post as a participant, leave facilitator notes, or trigger an AI pass without leaving the shared room.
collaborationPanel.aiActionsBody: Summary condenses the current shared discussion, evaluation highlights strengths and gaps, and follow-up proposes the next questions worth asking.
collaborationPanel.membersBody: Presence, current speaker, and typing state stay visible so the room feels like one live group discussion.
collaborationPanel.memberDirectoryBody: Review every member, presence state, and current role. When your role allows it, you can manage members here.
collaborationPanel.memberReadonlyBody: Your current role can view members but cannot change roles or remove people.
collaborationPanel.noMembersVisible: No members are visible right now.
collaborationPanel.invitesBody: Invite links open the same shared history. New participants arrive with context, not an empty thread.
collaborationPanel.attachmentsBody: Upload shared evidence, notes, images, or documents so they can be cited in the room and the knowledge layer.
collaborationPanel.invitesPrivateRoom: Private rooms do not generate shareable invite links. Switch the room to invite-only if collaborators need to join.
collaborationPanel.visibilityPublicBody: This is a public room. It can appear in dashboard discovery surfaces so collaborators can decide whether to open it.
collaborationPanel.visibilityInviteBody: This is an invite-only room. The history stays available, but only invited participants can join the workspace.
collaborationPanel.visibilityPrivateBody: This is a private room. It stays out of public discovery and does not generate shareable invites.
collaborationPanel.joinPublicRoomBody: You are viewing this room as a public visitor. Join first so the workspace can bind your profile, posting seat, uploads, and presence state.
collaborationPanel.joinedPublicRoom: Public room joined. Your profile, avatar, and presence now follow your current local identity.
collaborationPanel.publicViewerHint: You are looking at a public room preview. Join before posting, uploading attachments, or syncing your own presence.
collaborationPanel.invitesModeratorOnly: Only the current room host or facilitator can manage invite links.
collaborationPanel.identityLocked: Your current identity is not bound to an operable room seat in this workspace.
collaborationPanel.singleUserBody: Use this solo workspace as a normal AI conversation space first, then decide later whether to publish it as a shared room.
collaborationPanel.singleUserPlaceholder: Enter the question, argument, evidence, or context you want AI to analyze...
collaborationPanel.singleUserAiBody: Summary condenses the current solo thread, evaluation highlights strengths and gaps, and follow-up proposes the next questions worth asking.
collaborationPanel.singleUserWorkspaceBody: This remains a private personal AI workspace until you decide to open it as a shared room.
collaborationPanel.singleUserPublished: This solo workspace is now invite-only and ready for collaborators to join.
collaborationPanel.singleUserSharedHint: This workspace is already shareable. Continue through invites when you want other people to join.
collaborationPanel.presenceAutoManaged: Presence is updated automatically from page visibility, network state, and sync activity: {status}
collaborationPanel.replyPendingBody: The model is drafting a reply from the current workspace context.
collaborationPanel.regenerateHint: You can switch models within the current provider before regenerating this reply.
collaborationPanel.replyInterruptedEmpty: This reply was stopped before any text arrived.
collaborationPanel.imageNotSupported: The current AI model does not support image analysis. Images will be stored as reference only.
collaborationPanel.switchProviderHint: Switch to a provider that supports images (OpenAI, Claude, Gemini).
collaborationPanel.attachmentContextOnly: Workspace uploads stay attached to the session; only parseable text and text-like document content currently flows straight into the live chat path, while images, audio, video, and binary files remain reference context.
collaborationPanel.runSummaryHint: Summarize the current discussion, conclusions, and next steps.
collaborationPanel.runEvaluationHint: Assess the strength, gaps, and quality of the current answer or argument.
collaborationPanel.runFollowupHint: Generate next questions, follow-up prompts, or concrete action ideas.
collaborationPanel.sendAsCurrentIdentity: Messages are sent as the participant bound to your current local identity.
collaborationPanel.pendingAttachments: {count} attachment context item(s) will go out with your next message.
collaborationPanel.uploadImageUnsupported: The current workspace chat path does not yet send images as native model input. Images stay as reference attachments.
collaborationPanel.uploadFileUnsupported: Direct chat currently accepts only parseable text/document files.
collaborationPanel.imageReferenceQueued: The image is staged in the composer and will be sent as reference context with your next message.
collaborationPanel.documentReferenceQueued: The file is staged in the composer and will be sent as reference context with your next message.
collaborationPanel.attachmentsComposeHint: Uploads now live in the main composer. Images and files stage there first and are only sent when you press Send.
collaborationPanel.referenceAttachmentHint: Some staged attachments still travel only as reference context and are not sent as native multimodal input.

project.sampleProtectedHint: This bundled sample is for browsing and demo use. Changes, saves, deletes, uploads, and messages are not written.
project.sampleMutationBlocked: This sample is read-only for display; changes cannot be saved.
project.sampleSummaryProtected: Sample workspaces cannot run summary tasks.
project.deleteConfirm: Delete this project, its collaboration history, attachments, and knowledge snapshots?
project.deleteSampleProtected: Sample projects are protected and cannot be deleted.
project.workspaceReadOnly: Your current identity can only view this workspace and cannot edit it directly.
project.workspaceArchivedReadonly: This workspace is archived and read-only. Restore it before making changes again.
project.workspaceAiLocked: Your current identity cannot run AI analysis for this project yet. Join the room first or switch to a project member profile.
project.deleteDismissHint: Click delete again to collapse this confirmation.

newProject.title: Create a new discussion workspace
newProject.subtitle: Start from a structured room brief so future providers can summarize, evaluate, and extend the discussion cleanly.
newProject.createHint: You can refine participants, room presence, argument nodes, and provider settings after the workspace is created.
newProject.templatesHint: Pick a richer starting pattern, or begin from a clean room and shape the workflow yourself.
newProject.templateDeleteConfirm: Delete this template? Existing projects will not be affected.
newProject.soloSubtitle: Start with a private personal AI thread first, then open it up as a formal shared room when you need collaborators.
newProject.soloVisibilityHint: Solo workspaces start private. Open them from inside the workspace when you are ready to invite others.
newProject.soloCreateHint: After creation, you can use it like a normal AI chat workspace with history, attachments, summaries, evaluations, and a later path into a shared room.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure language, appearance, workspace defaults, provider adapters, collaboration behavior, privacy expectations, and the knowledge / graph pipeline from one control surface.
settings.appearanceHint: Switch among four UI languages and theme modes without changing the underlying discussion data.
settings.defaultsHint: These defaults are applied when creating a new discussion room locally.
settings.providerHint: Dialectica stays provider-agnostic while exposing server-side HTTP adapters, model catalogs, credential rules, and local fallback behavior.
settings.highlightKeywords: Highlight search keywords in the timeline
settings.aiFocusTopicsPlaceholder: Comma-separated topics the AI should prioritize
settings.quickRepliesHint: Pre-defined phrases you can insert quickly during conversations.
settings.clickToChange: Click a key, then press your new shortcut
settings.emailNotificationsHint: External email provider is not configured. Email-worthy events are recorded as local notifications only.
settings.emailEnabled: Record email-worthy events as local notifications
settings.onNewMember: Notify when a new member joins
settings.onAiSummary: Notify when AI completes a summary
settings.onRoomArchived: Notify when a room is archived
settings.collaborationHint: These settings control polling, invite creation, presence visibility, and the collaboration scaffolding used before full realtime transport is enabled.
settings.sessionAutoStart: Auto-start the session when participants are present
settings.sessionAutoArchive: Archive inactive sessions during lifecycle sync
settings.showPresenceIndicators: Show presence indicators in the UI
settings.privacyHint: These controls document local key handling, analysis behavior, and diagnostic sharing before hosted deployment or cloud sync is introduced.
settings.storeApiKeysLocally: Keep saved API keys in this local profile
settings.loadSamples: Bundled sample data is injected automatically when missing, while saved/imported projects stay in the local data directory.
settings.dataManagementHint: Export or import all your projects, settings, and data.
settings.aiSessionCleanupHint: Automatically remove inactive personal AI workspace sessions after a fixed period. This only applies to your own single-user AI chats.
settings.aiSessionCleanupEnabled: Enable automatic cleanup for personal AI sessions
settings.aiSessionCleanupScope: This cleanup only affects your own personal AI workspace sessions. It does not delete shared rooms or other users' records.
settings.exportAllDataHint: Download a JSON backup of all projects and settings.
settings.importDataHint: Restore projects and settings from a backup file.
settings.importConfirm: This will import data from the backup file. Existing projects with the same ID will be updated from the backup. Continue?
settings.aboutHint: Dialectica is an open-source, AI-ready workspace for structured debate, discussion, meeting capture, collaboration, knowledge extraction, and graph-ready analysis.
settings.keyPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep this provider in scaffold mode.
settings.saveHelper: Every change stays in the current draft first. Language, theme, avatar, provider, and collaboration preferences only become active after you press Save Settings.
settings.appliedImmediately: Language, theme, avatar, provider, and model settings now all follow the same rule: edit first, save second, apply third.
settings.languageApplied: Interface language applied and locale route updated.
settings.defaultBehaviorHint: Default scenario, export format, room visibility, sync transport, and automatic analysis flags are reused when you create or import a workspace.
settings.providerStageHint: Mainstream providers now expose real server-side HTTP adapter code. Live vendor calls still require valid credentials, reachable endpoints, and models that match your account.
settings.credentialsPersisted: API keys are stored in the active local profile secret store, so this provider can be tested and reused after refresh.
settings.credentialsNotPersisted: If local retention is disabled, saved API keys are cleared when settings are written and must be entered again later.
settings.analyticsHelp: Local-only mode keeps automatic summaries and evaluations active on save/import. Manual/export mode preserves the structure but waits for explicit AI tasks.
settings.preferServerKeys: Prefer server environment keys over locally entered keys
settings.allowFallbackToScaffold: Fallback to scaffold output when live HTTP calls fail
settings.showSystemEvents: Show join, invite, and upload system events
settings.uploadPreferencesHint: These preferences determine which attachment types are accepted and whether uploaded files remain on local disk.
settings.knowledgeHint: Control how discussions become reusable knowledge nodes, evidence links, unresolved questions, and graph relationships.
settings.autoExtractOnSave: Extract knowledge when projects are saved or imported
settings.autoExtractAfterAiTask: Extract knowledge after AI analysis tasks
settings.includeAttachmentsAsEvidence: Treat attachments as evidence / reference sources
settings.includeUnresolvedQuestions: Promote unresolved questions into knowledge nodes
settings.autoGenerateGraphLinks: Generate graph links for unresolved threads and related nodes
settings.knowledgeBehaviorHint: These settings determine when Dialectica turns structured discussion output into reusable knowledge objects and graph links.
settings.profileHint: Set the display identity used for locally created rooms, invite joins, and shared room updates.
settings.restoreRecommendedHint: Apply Dialectica's recommended language, appearance, collaboration, and analysis defaults for a clean starting point.
settings.recommendedApplied: Recommended defaults were restored and applied.
settings.providerModeLockedHint: Provider mode is tied to the selected provider. Switch providers instead of forcing a mismatched runtime mode.
settings.modelScopeHint: Only models published in the active provider catalog can be selected and saved.
settings.avatarHint: Choose one of the default identity marks or upload a small avatar for collaboration, participant lists, and reports.
settings.avatarUploadHint: PNG, JPG, WEBP, or GIF up to 512 KB.
settings.avatarInvalidType: Avatar must be a PNG, JPG, WEBP, or GIF image.
settings.avatarTooLarge: Avatar image must be 512 KB or smaller.
settings.avatarUpdated: Avatar preview updated. Save settings to persist it.
settings.avatarRemoved: Custom avatar removed. Save settings to keep the preset only.
settings.localProviderConfigHint: This provider currently runs with a local or reserved adapter mode, so remote credential fields stay hidden here.
settings.disabledAdapterHint: The current provider / mode cannot execute remote AI calls. This is a status state, not a normal provider catalog. Switch to a provider with executable models to enable AI again.
settings.disabledAdapterNoModel: No executable model is available in this state.
settings.preferencesHint: These preferences directly change timeline density, keyword highlighting, graph density, and presence visibility in the workspace.
settings.customThemeHint: Save a personal accent palette for the current workspace shell. It updates buttons, graph highlights, hero accents, and focused UI states.
settings.customThemePresetSaved: The custom theme was saved to your theme library.
settings.customThemePresetRemoved: The custom theme was removed from your theme library.
settings.avatarCustomLockHint: A custom avatar is active. Remove it to re-enable the default avatar presets below.
settings.providerKeyStoredHint: This profile already has a saved key for the provider: {maskedKey}. Leave the field blank to keep using it, or enter a new key to replace it.
settings.providerKeyCleared: The saved key for this provider has been cleared.
settings.unsavedLeavePrompt: You still have unsaved settings changes. Are you sure you want to leave this page?
settings.changesDiscarded: Unsaved changes were discarded and the page returned to the last saved state.
settings.changesPending: Changes are staged in draft form and will only apply after you save settings.
settings.providerDraftHint: Provider, model, base URL, and API key changes stay in draft form until you save settings.
settings.providerTestStagedHint: The connection test result is still a draft and will be kept only after you save settings.
settings.manualGraphGenerationHint: Knowledge graphs are created only when you trigger generation manually. Creating, opening, or refreshing a workspace will not generate graphs automatically.
settings.autoSummaryThresholdHint: Set how many new messages should accumulate before auto summary runs by default.
settings.assistiveSummaryThresholdHint: Assistive mode is multi-user only: this is the base review count, and AI saves a summary only for batches with enough signal.
settings.latestAiHistoryLimitHint: Used only when recent AI interventions are retained.
settings.summaryHistoryRetentionHint: Used only when summary history is capped.

provider.description: Dialectica keeps the provider layer model-agnostic. Mock analysis works today, while real adapters can be introduced later without changing the workspace structure.

mock.label: Mock Rule Engine
mock.description: Deterministic local analysis for smoke tests, demos, and offline validation.

disabled.label: Disabled adapter state
disabled.description: Current state when the active provider / mode cannot execute remote calls. Switch to an execution-capable provider to re-enable AI.

openai.label: OpenAI
openai.description: Server-side HTTP adapter for the current official OpenAI GPT-5.2, GPT-5, GPT-4.1, and reasoning families.

gemini.label: Gemini
gemini.description: Server-side HTTP adapter for current Gemini 2.5 production models and Gemini 3 preview families.

grok.label: Grok
grok.description: Server-side HTTP adapter for xAI Grok 4 and fast reasoning families.

claude.label: Claude
claude.description: Server-side HTTP adapter for the latest Anthropic Claude catalog.

deepseek.label: DeepSeek
deepseek.description: Server-side HTTP adapter for the official DeepSeek Chat and Reasoner models.

doubao.label: Doubao
doubao.description: Server-side HTTP adapter for the Doubao 1.6 family and custom Ark endpoint IDs.

qwen.label: Qwen
qwen.description: Server-side HTTP adapter for the current Qwen / Model Studio production aliases.

importExport.title: Import and export
importExport.subtitle: Bring in JSON, TXT, or Markdown transcripts. Export raw data, transcript text, Markdown reports, and printable summaries.

report.title: Printable Report
report.subtitle: A print-friendly snapshot of the room brief, participant roster, issue tracker, collaboration state, and AI-ready summary panels.
report.diagnosticsHidden: Diagnostic room and provider details are hidden in this report because diagnostic export is disabled in Settings.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.description: Use these shortcuts to navigate faster.
shortcuts.globalSearchHint: Type to search across all projects and pages

errors.importFailed: Import failed. Please check the file format.
errors.connectionFailed: Connection test failed. Please verify the provider configuration.

emptyStates.summary: No summary available yet. Save the workspace to refresh analysis.

knowledge.title: Knowledge Hub
knowledge.subtitle: Turn meetings, debates, discussions, and AI dialogues into reusable knowledge nodes with provenance, tags, topics, and relationship edges.
knowledge.graphSubtitle: Explore how topics, viewpoints, arguments, evidence, conflicts, and recommendations connect across Dialectica. Zoom in for one project, or step back to compare several projects at once.
knowledge.empty: No knowledge entries are available yet.
knowledge.deleteNodeConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this knowledge node and its relations?

projectPanel.title: Project knowledge snapshot
projectPanel.subtitle: Review reusable knowledge extracted from this discussion, then jump into the cross-project hub or graph.

filters.title: Knowledge filters
filters.subtitle: Search and filter the knowledge base by query, category, topic, and source project.
filters.searchPlaceholder: Search titles, summaries, topics, or tags

knowledge.scopeProjectBody: This graph is isolated to one project so the local reasoning chain stays readable and easier to inspect.
knowledge.scopeCrossProjectBody: This graph compares knowledge across projects. Use it to spot overlap, tension, and recurring themes, then drop into a project-scoped graph for a cleaner reading.
knowledge.projectClustersBody: Keep one project isolated by default, or pivot into cross-project mode when you need a broader thematic map.
knowledge.graphLegendBody: Zoom, drag, and focus a node to see why nearby topics, arguments, evidence, and conclusions are connected.
knowledge.graphIsolatedHint: Project-scoped graphs stay cleaner and are the default way to inspect one room's reasoning chain.
knowledge.graphCrossProjectHint: Cross-project mode is intentionally broader. Use it to compare several projects, then jump into a single project graph for a focused reading.
knowledge.graphWheelHint: Use the mouse wheel inside the graph to zoom without scrolling the whole page.
knowledge.relationInspectorBody: Open any relation marker to see why two nodes were linked, what the relation means, and which reasoning step produced it.
knowledge.relationInspectorEmpty: Select a relation marker to inspect why the edge exists.
knowledge.nodeFocusIdleHint: Select a node in the graph first. This panel will then show its source project, topics, and nearby relations before you inspect individual edges.
knowledge.relationDerivedLabel: This explanation comes from Dialectica's structured analysis and provenance rules. With a live provider, the reasoning chain can be refined further.
knowledge.selectionHintProject: Keep the current project selected, then optionally add another project before switching into cross-project comparison.
knowledge.selectionHintCrossProject: Choose which projects stay in the comparison set, then apply the shared graph scope.
knowledge.generateGraphSuccess: A relation graph was generated from the current project content.
knowledge.graph3dHint: Drag to orbit · scroll to zoom
knowledge.myGraphsHint: Generate, manage, and share your own knowledge graphs from project discussions.
knowledge.graphGeneratingBody: This graph is still being generated in the background. You can stay here and the page will update automatically once it is ready.
knowledge.deleteGraphGroupConfirm: Delete this entire version history ({count} versions)?
knowledge.graphGroupDeleted: The entire graph version history was deleted.
knowledge.keepLatestGraphConfirm: Delete {count} older graph versions and keep only the latest one?
knowledge.keptLatestGraph: Older graph versions were deleted. The latest version was kept.
knowledge.deleteProjectClusterConfirm: Delete the project knowledge graph for “{title}”? This also removes the underlying project and its knowledge snapshots.
knowledge.projectClusterDeleted: Deleted “{title}” and its linked knowledge graph.
knowledge.projectClustersDeleteSelectedConfirm: Delete the {count} selected project clusters? This also removes the underlying projects and their knowledge snapshots.
knowledge.projectClustersDeleteSnoozeHint: You can restore the confirmation prompt later from the project clusters panel.
knowledge.projectClustersDeleteConfirmRestored: Project cluster delete confirmation was restored.
knowledge.noUserGraphs: You haven't generated any knowledge graphs yet. Click 'Generate graph' to create one from your project discussions.
knowledge.graphQualityHint: Graph quality, structure stability, and level of detail may vary across AI models. For critical graphs, use a model with stronger structured-output reliability.
knowledge.crossAnalysisHint: Select 2+ graphs to find shared concepts, conflicting viewpoints, and supporting conclusions.
knowledge.analysisGoalPlaceholder: Analysis goal (optional, e.g. 'Find common themes between these discussions')
knowledge.crossAnalysisFailed: Unable to complete cross-graph analysis right now. Please review the selected graphs and try again.
knowledge.estimatedTime: Estimated time: ~{time} seconds for {count} project(s). Generation runs in the background.
knowledge.sampleGraphGenerationDisabled: Sample projects are for demonstration only and cannot generate new knowledge graphs.

themePresetDescriptions.dialectica: Balanced ink-blue and brass accents tuned for everyday debate, meeting, and knowledge work.
themePresetDescriptions.paper: Calmer neutral surfaces for long-form review, reporting, and meeting synthesis.
themePresetDescriptions.midnight: Cooler blue-green contrast for graph work, collaboration, and dense analysis sessions.
themePresetDescriptions.custom: Save your own Dialectica accent trio for buttons, graph highlights, and homepage atmosphere.

avatarDescriptions.ember: Deep ember copper for the lead voice.
avatarDescriptions.harbor: Teal harbor tones for calm facilitation.
avatarDescriptions.plum: Vivid plum for counterpoints and debate.
avatarDescriptions.graphite: Neutral graphite for moderators and reviewers.
avatarDescriptions.sunrise: Rose-gold morning tones with lighter energy.

assistant.title: Personal AI workspace
assistant.subtitle: Use Dialectica like a private AI app first, while still keeping attachments, context, and a later path to publish the thread as a shared room.
assistant.personalWorkspaceHint: This starts as a private one-person AI workspace. Build up context, files, and chat history here before opening it to collaborators.
assistant.historyBody: Each conversation is stored as a locally persistent personal AI thread that you can resume or publish later.
assistant.emptyHistory: No personal AI conversations yet. Start a new chat first.
assistant.emptyConversation: No messages yet. Ask a question, attach context, or start a fresh AI conversation.
assistant.publishedWorkspace: This personal AI workspace is now an invite-only shared room.
assistant.privateWorkspaceBody: This conversation is still private and only visible to your local profile.
assistant.sharedWorkspaceBody: This workspace has already been opened as a shared room. Continue multi-user collaboration in the formal workspace view.
assistant.noSavedKeyBody: The current provider does not have a saved API key yet. Save a valid configuration in Settings before chatting directly here.
assistant.sharedModeRedirectHint: This thread is no longer single-user. You can still review it here, but ongoing collaboration should continue in the shared workspace.
assistant.attachmentNoteHint: Attachment from the personal AI workspace
assistant.uploadImageUnsupported: The current model does not support image input.
assistant.uploadVideoUnsupported: The current model does not support video input.
assistant.uploadAudioUnsupported: Audio input is not enabled for the current model yet.
assistant.uploadFileUnsupported: Direct chat currently accepts only parseable text/document files.
assistant.uploadReady: The attachment has been added to the current chat context.
assistant.pendingAttachments: {count} attachment context item(s) will be sent with your next message.
assistant.inputPlaceholder: Type the question, task, context, or draft you want the AI to continue from…
assistant.uploadDisabledByModel: This model does not expose attachment input here
assistant.emptyTitle: Start your first personal AI conversation
assistant.emptyBody: This route is not a multi-user debate room by default. It starts as your local AI workspace first.
assistant.sessionDeleteQueued: The session has been permanently deleted.
assistant.sessionDeleted: The session has been permanently deleted.
assistant.historyRetentionHint: Normal sessions are not deleted automatically. Archived sessions stay in Archive. Deleted sessions are permanently removed immediately.
assistant.archivedReadonlyBody: Archived sessions are now read-only. Restore the session before you continue chatting.
assistant.pendingCleanupReadonlyBody: This session has been deleted and is now in its 30-day cleanup window. You can review or restore it, but you cannot keep chatting until it is restored.
assistant.readonlySendBlocked: This session is read-only. Restore it before sending more messages.
assistant.readonlyUploadBlocked: This session is read-only. Restore it before adding more attachments.
assistant.richMediaContextOnly: This chat currently treats images, audio, and video as attachment context only and does not send them as native multimodal input to the model.
assistant.replyPendingBody: The model is working through the current context and attachments.
assistant.regenerateHint: You can switch models within the current provider before regenerating this reply.
assistant.replyInterruptedEmpty: This reply was stopped before any text arrived.
assistant.imageAttachmentNote: Reference image from the AI workspace
assistant.documentAttachmentNote: Reference document from the AI workspace
assistant.imageReferenceQueued: The image is staged in the composer and will be sent as reference context with your next message.
assistant.documentReferenceQueued: The file is staged in the composer and will be sent as reference context with your next message.
assistant.referenceAttachmentHint: Some staged attachments still travel only as reference context and are not sent as native multimodal input.
assistant.graphGenerating: Knowledge graph generation started. You will be notified when it is ready.
